mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) reflect expanding supply capacity and broader economic momentum, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return is non-positive in more than 60% of months when INDPRO increases relative to the prior month, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate above 6.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that erode consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return is negative in less than 55% of instances when the unemployment rate exceeds 6.5%, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the real interest rate (proxied by FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation) turns negative, it creates an accommodative financial condition that lowers discount rates and encourages equity investment, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: real rate < 0% (FEDFUNDS minus annual CPIAUCSL change)
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return is not positive in at least two-thirds of episodes where the real rate is negative, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound regime where FEDFUNDS is at or below 0.25%, market participants anticipate unconventional easing measures such as quantitative easing, which compress risk premiums and result in positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the median 60-day forward return when FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.25% is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p-value < 0.10), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A robust quarterly GDP growth rate above 3% (annualized) as measured by GDPC1 signals a booming economy with strong aggregate demand, which drives corporate profits higher and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return is not positive in at least 60% of quarters following GDP growth above 3%, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer income and confidence, supporting corporate sales and leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the directional accuracy of monthly PAYEMS increases predicting 20-day forward SPX returns is below 55% over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When both housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) rise above their respective 6-month moving averages, it signals synchronized consumer demand and housing market strength that drives economic optimism and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return is not positive in at least 70% of months when both conditions hold, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing crisis regime defined by low housing starts (below 600K units) and high unemployment (above 9%), consumer wealth and confidence are depressed, leading to persistent drag on corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return is not negative in at least 60% of overlapping quarters that satisfy the trigger condition, this hypothesis is rejected.
